behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: request-builder/config/taxonomy serialization must be wired into the API client call. A unit test of a pure formatter could pass while the real call path omits labels. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real network/OS effects; a stub API client capturing outbound payload reproduces the failure. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no dependence on model judgment or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module with a fake in-process API client that records outbound request payload. Invoke classify() with a taxonomy and assert the captured request contains the full taxonomy label list (exact set, no missing labels).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: The real failure can be at module wiring: API response parsing + taxonomy validation + public return path. A unit test on normalization alone may miss bypass/regression in the classify() flow. Rejected System: no real HTTP/OS needed; stubbed API response is sufficient. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: correctness here is deterministic post-processing, not model quality or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API adapter that returns an out-of-taxonomy label (for example "medical"). Call classify() and assert returned value is exactly "unknown" and never the raw unexpected label.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly a normalization determinism property on identical input, i.e., one local function/object boundary with no I/O. Rejected Integration: no module handoff is required by this behavior text. Rejected higher levels: no real infra, no live model dependence, no multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with the same raw API payload fixture and taxonomy; assert output label is identical across runs.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live AI API calls and consistency relative to provider output changes; this depends on real model/provider behavior and potential non-determinism. A mock would hide these failures. Rejected Workflow: despite repeated calls, this is not a multi-stage agent pipeline with distinct agentic steps; it is repeated single-step classification calls.
behaviors.test_description: Run N live classifications for the same document+taxonomy, storing raw provider response and final label each time. Assert: when raw provider response is unchanged between two runs, final label is unchanged; if raw response differs, label change is permitted.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is request-construction to dispatch handoff: budget logic must actually gate outbound calls. Unit tests on token-budget helper alone can miss that classify() dispatches anyway or applies wrong policy. Rejected System: real network/OS not required; fake dispatch target is sufficient. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: this tests pre-dispatch control logic, not model output quality or chained agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small token budget and a document that exceeds it. With a fake API client, verify classify() either truncates according to policy before send or rejects without send, and that dispatched payload (if any) satisfies budget.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real provider call and provider-reported usage metadata for actual model processing. Stubbed usage values can always pass and hide provider/model-specific over-budget behavior. Rejected Workflow: single call budget validation, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live classification on representative documents with production tokenizer/model settings; assert provider-reported token usage field is <= configured per-call budget for each case.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is retry-orchestration logic across module + API adapter + backoff policy. In-process fakes can reproduce 429/rate-limit signal and verify wait/retry behavior. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real network timing or OS primitives to expose failure. Rejected Agentic: correctness is infrastructure around the API call (retry handling), not dependent on model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API adapter scripted to return rate-limit error first, then success. Assert classify() performs a delay/backoff and retries, ultimately returning success instead of surfacing initial rate-limit error.
